1st original version
#libs
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(microbiome)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(PNWColors)
library(gridExtra)
```
# read phyloseq and define parameters
```{r}
#ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
meta <- ps@sam_data
# make Batch as factor
meta$Batch <- as.factor(meta$Batch)
ps_obj <- ps
level <- 'Species'
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
measure <- 'Shannon'
method <- 'wilcox.test'

# Prepare the phyloseq object
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)
ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)

# Define the columns to keep for comparisons
categorical_vars <- names(meta)[sapply(meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age', "Group",'BMI', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 
                                                'Weight', "SampleID"))
# convert all vars to factors
for (col in categorical_vars) {
  if (col %in% colnames(meta) && (is.character(meta[[col]]) || is.integer(meta[[col]]))) {
    ps_obj@sam_data[[col]] <- as.factor(ps_obj@sam_data[[col]])
  }
}

# Get unique groups and timepoints
groups <- as.factor(sort(unique(ps_obj@sam_data$Group)))  # Sort to ensure consistent order
timepoints <- as.factor(sort(unique(ps_obj@sam_data$Timepoint)))

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "/home/sonec_iv/alpha_diversity_by_variable_MAGs"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Define color palettes as requested
# Group A: Red colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupA_colors <- c("-1" = "#FF9999",  # Light red for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#FF3333")  # Original red for Timepoint -2

# Group B: Blue colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupB_colors <- c("-1" = "#99CCFF",  # Light blue for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#3366FF")  # Original blue for Timepoint -2

# Group C: Green colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupC_colors <- c("-1" = "#99FF99",  # Light green for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#33CC33")  # Original green for Timepoint -2

# For group comparisons (plots 1 & 2): Use light colors for -1, original for -2
group_palette_time1 <- c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#99CCFF", "C" = "#99FF99")  # Light colors for Timepoint -1
group_palette_time2 <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")  # Original colors for Timepoint -2

# For timepoint comparisons (plots 3-5)
timepoint_palettes <- list(
  "A" = groupA_colors,
  "B" = groupB_colors,
  "C" = groupC_colors
)
```



# orig function to plot alpha diversity with custom comparisons
```{r}
plot_alpha_div_custom <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    color_var = NULL,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    palette = NULL,
    title = ""
) {
  if (is.null(color_var)) {
    color_var <- group_var
  }
  
  # Generate boxplot of alpha diversity
  p <- plot_richness(ps_obj, x = group_var, color = color_var, measures = measure)
  
  # Add comparisons if provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons)) {
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisons, 
                                 method = "wilcox.test", 
                                 label = "p.signif", 
                                 size = 5,
                                 step.increase = 0.1,
                                 tip.length = 0.02)
  }
  
  # Add palette if provided
  if (!is.null(palette)) {
    p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = palette)
  }
  
  # Add title
  if (title != "") {
    p <- p + ggtitle(title)
  }
  
  return(p)
}
```

# debug function
```{r}
# Modified version of your original function with debug prints
plot_alpha_div_custom <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    color_var = NULL,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    palette = NULL,
    title = ""
) {
  if (is.null(color_var)) {
    color_var <- group_var
  }
  
  cat("\n=== DEBUG INFO ===\n")
  cat("Group variable:", group_var, "\n")
  cat("Color variable:", color_var, "\n")
  cat("Measure:", measure, "\n")
  
  # Generate boxplot of alpha diversity
  p <- plot_richness(ps_obj, x = group_var, color = color_var, measures = measure)
  
  # Print the structure of the plot
  cat("\nPlot structure after plot_richness:\n")
  print(str(p$data, max.level = 2))
  
  cat("\nAesthetics mapping:\n")
  print(p$mapping)
  
  cat("\nLayers in plot:\n")
  print(length(p$layers))
  
  # Add comparisons if provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons)) {
    cat("\nAdding comparisons:", paste(sapply(comparisons, paste, collapse=" vs "), collapse="; "), "\n")
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisons, 
                                 method = "wilcox.test", 
                                 label = "p.signif", 
                                 size = 5,
                                 step.increase = 0.1,
                                 tip.length = 0.02)
  }
  
  # Check plot data after adding stat_compare_means
  cat("\nPlot data structure after adding stat_compare_means:\n")
  print(str(p$data, max.level = 2))
  
  # Add palette if provided
  if (!is.null(palette)) {
    p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = palette)
  }
  
  # Add title
  if (title != "") {
    p <- p + ggtitle(title)
  }
  
  return(p)
}
```

#check meta
```{r}
check_factors <- function() {
  meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  cat("\n=== CHECKING FACTOR LEVELS ===\n")
  
  # Check Group
  cat("\nGroup variable:\n")
  cat("Class:", class(meta$Group), "\n")
  cat("Levels:", if(is.factor(meta$Group)) paste(levels(meta$Group), collapse=", ") else "Not a factor", "\n")
  cat("Unique values:", paste(unique(meta$Group), collapse=", "), "\n")
  
  # Check Timepoint
  cat("\nTimepoint variable:\n")
  cat("Class:", class(meta$Timepoint), "\n")
  
  # Check a sample categorical variable
  sample_var <- categorical_vars[1]
  if(length(categorical_vars) > 0) {
    cat("\nSample categorical variable (", sample_var, "):\n", sep="")
    cat("Class:", class(meta[[sample_var]]), "\n")
  }
  
  # Check for NAs in Group
  cat("\nNA values in Group:", sum(is.na(meta$Group)), "\n")
}

check_factors()
```

# upd version (WORKS)
```{r}
# Function to plot alpha diversity with custom comparisons - WORKING VERSION
plot_alpha_div_custom <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    color_var = NULL,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    palette = NULL,
    title = ""
) {
  if (is.null(color_var)) {
    color_var <- group_var
  }
  
  # Calculate alpha diversity manually
  alpha_df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
  colnames(alpha_df) <- measure
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variables to factors if they're not already
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[color_var]])) {
    meta_df[[color_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[color_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Create the base plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes_string(x = group_var, y = measure, color = color_var)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = group_var,
         y = paste(measure, "Diversity"))
  
  # Add comparisons if provided - REVERTED TO STANDARD STARS
  if (!is.null(comparisons)) {
    # Ensure comparisons are in the right format (as characters, not integers)
    comparisons <- lapply(comparisons, function(x) {
      as.character(x)
    })
    
    # Add standard significance stars (no p-values)
    p <- p + ggpubr::stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = comparisons, 
      method = "wilcox.test", 
      label = "p.signif",  # Just stars
      size = 5,
      step.increase = 0.1,
      tip.length = 0.02
    )
  }
  
  # Add palette if provided
  if (!is.null(palette)) {
    p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = palette)
  }
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = plot_df))
}

# Function to perform statistical tests and save results (KEEP THIS - it's working!)
save_statistics <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    filename
) {
  # Calculate alpha diversity
  alpha_df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
  colnames(alpha_df) <- measure
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variable to factor if needed
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Save alpha diversity data
  alpha_data_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_alpha_data.csv")
  write.csv(plot_df, alpha_data_file, row.names = TRUE)
  cat("  Saved alpha diversity data:", alpha_data_file, "\n")
  
  # Perform statistical tests if comparisons are provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons) && length(comparisons) > 0) {
    stats_results <- data.frame()
    
    for (i in seq_along(comparisons)) {
      comp <- comparisons[[i]]
      group1 <- comp[1]
      group2 <- comp[2]
      
      # Extract data for the two groups
      data1 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group1, measure]
      data2 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group2, measure]
      
      # Perform Wilcoxon test
      if (length(data1) > 0 && length(data2) > 0) {
        test_result <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        
        stats_row <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
          Group1 = group1,
          Group2 = group2,
          N_Group1 = length(data1),
          N_Group2 = length(data2),
          Mean_Group1 = mean(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
          Mean_Group2 = mean(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
          SD_Group1 = sd(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
          SD_Group2 = sd(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
          W_statistic = test_result$statistic,
          P_value = test_result$p.value,
          P_value_rounded = round(test_result$p.value, 4),
          Significance = ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                                ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                       ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
        )
        
        stats_results <- rbind(stats_results, stats_row)
      }
    }
    
    # Save statistics results
    if (nrow(stats_results) > 0) {
      stats_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_stats.csv")
      write.csv(stats_results, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("  Saved statistics results:", stats_file, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(plot_df)
}

# Function to perform statistical tests and save results
save_statistics <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    filename
) {
  # Calculate alpha diversity - with error handling
  alpha_df <- tryCatch({
    df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("  WARNING: microbiome::alpha() failed, using estimate_richness()\n")
    alpha_est <- estimate_richness(ps_obj, measures = measure)
    df <- data.frame(alpha_est)
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  })
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variable to factor if needed
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Save alpha diversity data
  alpha_data_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_alpha_data.csv")
  write.csv(plot_df, alpha_data_file, row.names = TRUE)
  cat("  Saved alpha diversity data:", alpha_data_file, "\n")
  
  # Perform statistical tests if comparisons are provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons) && length(comparisons) > 0) {
    stats_results <- data.frame()
    
    for (i in seq_along(comparisons)) {
      comp <- comparisons[[i]]
      group1 <- as.character(comp[1])
      group2 <- as.character(comp[2])
      
      # Check if groups exist in data
      if (!group1 %in% plot_df[[group_var]] || !group2 %in% plot_df[[group_var]]) {
        cat("  WARNING: Skipping comparison", group1, "vs", group2, "- groups not in data\n")
        next
      }
      
      # Extract data for the two groups
      data1 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group1, measure]
      data2 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group2, measure]
      
      # Perform Wilcoxon test
      if (length(data1) > 1 && length(data2) > 1) {
        test_result <- tryCatch({
          wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat("  ERROR in wilcox.test for", group1, "vs", group2, ":", e$message, "\n")
          NULL
        })
        
        if (!is.null(test_result)) {
          stats_row <- data.frame(
            Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
            Group1 = group1,
            Group2 = group2,
            N_Group1 = length(data1),
            N_Group2 = length(data2),
            Mean_Group1 = mean(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
            Mean_Group2 = mean(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
            SD_Group1 = sd(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
            SD_Group2 = sd(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
            W_statistic = test_result$statistic,
            P_value = test_result$p.value,
            P_value_rounded = round(test_result$p.value, 4),
            Significance = ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                                  ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                         ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
          )
          
          stats_results <- rbind(stats_results, stats_row)
        }
      } else {
        cat("  WARNING: Not enough data for comparison", group1, "vs", group2, "\n")
      }
    }
    
    # Save statistics results
    if (nrow(stats_results) > 0) {
      stats_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_stats.csv")
      write.csv(stats_results, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("  Saved statistics results:", stats_file, "\n")
    } else {
      cat("  WARNING: No statistical results to save\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(plot_df)
}

# In your main loop, add this at the beginning to handle Timepoint properly:
# Convert Timepoint to character first, then factor (since it's integer)
ps_obj@sam_data$Timepoint <- factor(as.character(ps_obj@sam_data$Timepoint))

# Also ensure Group is factor
ps_obj@sam_data$Group <- factor(ps_obj@sam_data$Group)

# And when creating time_comparisons, use characters:
time_comparisons <- list(c("-1", "-2"))


# Function to perform statistical tests and save results
save_statistics <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    filename
) {
  # Calculate alpha diversity
  alpha_df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
  colnames(alpha_df) <- measure
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variable to factor if needed
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Save alpha diversity data
  alpha_data_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_alpha_data.csv")
  write.csv(plot_df, alpha_data_file, row.names = TRUE)
  cat("  Saved alpha diversity data:", alpha_data_file, "\n")
  
  # Perform statistical tests if comparisons are provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons) && length(comparisons) > 0) {
    stats_results <- data.frame()
    
    for (i in seq_along(comparisons)) {
      comp <- comparisons[[i]]
      group1 <- comp[1]
      group2 <- comp[2]
      
      # Extract data for the two groups
      data1 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group1, measure]
      data2 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group2, measure]
      
      # Perform Wilcoxon test
      if (length(data1) > 0 && length(data2) > 0) {
        test_result <- wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        
        stats_row <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
          Group1 = group1,
          Group2 = group2,
          N_Group1 = length(data1),
          N_Group2 = length(data2),
          Mean_Group1 = mean(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
          Mean_Group2 = mean(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
          SD_Group1 = sd(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
          SD_Group2 = sd(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
          W_statistic = test_result$statistic,
          P_value = test_result$p.value,
          P_value_rounded = round(test_result$p.value, 4),
          Significance = ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                                ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                       ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
        )
        
        stats_results <- rbind(stats_results, stats_row)
      }
    }
    
    # Save statistics results
    if (nrow(stats_results) > 0) {
      stats_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_stats.csv")
      write.csv(stats_results, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("  Saved statistics results:", stats_file, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(plot_df)
}

ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
meta <- ps@sam_data
meta$Batch <- as.factor(meta$Batch)
ps_obj <- ps
level <- 'Species'
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
measure <- 'Shannon'
method <- 'wilcox.test'

# Prepare the phyloseq object
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)
ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)

# Define the columns to keep for comparisons
categorical_vars <- names(meta)[sapply(meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age', "Group",'BMI', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 
                                                'Weight', "SampleID"))
# convert all vars to factors
for (col in categorical_vars) {
  if (col %in% colnames(meta) && (is.character(meta[[col]]) || is.integer(meta[[col]]))) {
    ps_obj@sam_data[[col]] <- as.factor(ps_obj@sam_data[[col]])
  }
}

# Get unique groups and timepoints
groups <- as.factor(sort(unique(ps_obj@sam_data$Group)))  # Sort to ensure consistent order
timepoints <- as.factor(sort(unique(ps_obj@sam_data$Timepoint)))

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "/home/sonec_iv/alpha_diversity_by_variable_MAGs"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Define color palettes as requested
# Group A: Red colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupA_colors <- c("-1" = "#FF9999",  # Light red for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#FF3333")  # Original red for Timepoint -2

# Group B: Blue colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupB_colors <- c("-1" = "#99CCFF",  # Light blue for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#3366FF")  # Original blue for Timepoint -2

# Group C: Green colors (light for -1, original for -2)
groupC_colors <- c("-1" = "#99FF99",  # Light green for Timepoint -1
                   "-2" = "#33CC33")  # Original green for Timepoint -2

# For group comparisons (plots 1 & 2): Use light colors for -1, original for -2
group_palette_time1 <- c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#99CCFF", "C" = "#99FF99")  # Light colors for Timepoint -1
group_palette_time2 <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")  # Original colors for Timepoint -2

# For timepoint comparisons (plots 3-5)
timepoint_palettes <- list(
  "A" = groupA_colors,
  "B" = groupB_colors,
  "C" = groupC_colors
)


# Loop through each categorical variable
for (col in categorical_vars) {
  cat("\nProcessing variable:", col, "\n")
  
  # Create a subdirectory for this variable
  var_dir <- file.path(output_dir, col)
  dir.create(var_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  # Skip if not enough unique values
  if (length(unique(ps_obj@sam_data[[col]])) < 2) {
    cat("  Skipping - less than 2 unique values\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Store plots for this variable
  var_plots <- list()
  
  # PLOT 1: Groups A vs B vs C in Timepoint -1 (with light colors)
  cat("  Creating Plot 1: Groups in Timepoint -1\n")
  ps_time1 <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == "-1")
  
  if (nsamples(ps_time1) >= 2 && length(unique(ps_time1@sam_data$Group)) >= 2) {
    # Create comparisons between groups
    group_comparisons <- lapply(combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE), as.vector)
    
    # Save statistics data first
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_time1,
      group_var = "Group",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = group_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_1_groups_in_timepoint-1.png"))
    )
    
    # Create plot using the updated function
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_time1,
      group_var = "Group",
      color_var = "Group",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = group_comparisons,
      palette = group_palette_time1,
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nGroups comparison in Timepoint -1")
    )
    
    p1 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Group)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = group_palette_time1) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)  # Extra top margin for p-values
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[1]] <- p1
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_1_groups_in_timepoint-1.png")),
      plot = p1,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  # PLOT 2: Groups A vs B vs C in Timepoint -2 (with original colors)
  cat("  Creating Plot 2: Groups in Timepoint -2\n")
  ps_time2 <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == "-2")
  
  if (nsamples(ps_time2) >= 2 && length(unique(ps_time2@sam_data$Group)) >= 2) {
    # Save statistics data
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_time2,
      group_var = "Group",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = group_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_2_groups_in_timepoint-2.png"))
    )
    
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_time2,
      group_var = "Group",
      color_var = "Group",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = group_comparisons,
      palette = group_palette_time2,
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nGroups comparison in Timepoint -2")
    )
    
    p2 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Group)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = group_palette_time2) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[2]] <- p2
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_2_groups_in_timepoint-2.png")),
      plot = p2,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  # PLOT 3: Group A - Timepoint -1 (light red) vs -2 (red)
  cat("  Creating Plot 3: Group A timepoints\n")
  ps_groupA <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "A")
  
  if (nsamples(ps_groupA) >= 2 && length(unique(ps_groupA@sam_data$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
    time_comparisons <- list(c("-1", "-2"))
    
    # Save statistics data
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_groupA,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_3_groupA_timepoints.png"))
    )
    
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_groupA,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      color_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      palette = timepoint_palettes[["A"]],
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nGroup A: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
    )
    
    p3 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["A"]]) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[3]] <- p3
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_3_groupA_timepoints.png")),
      plot = p3,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  # PLOT 4: Group B - Timepoint -1 (light blue) vs -2 (blue)
  cat("  Creating Plot 4: Group B timepoints\n")
  ps_groupB <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "B")
  
  if (nsamples(ps_groupB) >= 2 && length(unique(ps_groupB@sam_data$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
    # Save statistics data
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_groupB,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_4_groupB_timepoints.png"))
    )
    
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_groupB,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      color_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      palette = timepoint_palettes[["B"]],
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nGroup B: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
    )
    
    p4 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["B"]]) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[4]] <- p4
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_4_groupB_timepoints.png")),
      plot = p4,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  # PLOT 5: Group C - Timepoint -1 (light green) vs -2 (green)
  cat("  Creating Plot 5: Group C timepoints\n")
  ps_groupC <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "C")
  
  if (nsamples(ps_groupC) >= 2 && length(unique(ps_groupC@sam_data$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
    # Save statistics data
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_groupC,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_5_groupC_timepoints.png"))
    )
    
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_groupC,
      group_var = "Timepoint",
      color_var = "Timepoint",
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = time_comparisons,
      palette = timepoint_palettes[["C"]],
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nGroup C: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
    )
    
    p5 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["C"]]) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[5]] <- p5
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_5_groupC_timepoints.png")),
      plot = p5,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  # PLOT 6: Alpha diversity by the categorical variable itself
  cat("  Creating Plot 6: Variable distribution\n")
  
  # Check if this variable has enough unique values to plot
  unique_vals <- unique(ps_obj@sam_data[[col]])
  if (length(unique_vals) >= 2) {
    # Create comparisons for the variable itself
    var_comparisons <- lapply(combn(unique_vals, 2, simplify = FALSE), as.vector)
    
    # Create a palette for this variable's levels
    n_colors <- length(unique_vals)
    if (n_colors <= 8) {
      var_palette <- pnw_palette("Sunset2", n_colors, type = "continuous")
    } else {
      var_palette <- pnw_palette("Sunset2", 8, type = "continuous")
      var_palette <- colorRampPalette(var_palette)(n_colors)
    }
    names(var_palette) <- unique_vals
    
    # Save statistics data
    stats_data <- save_statistics(
      ps_obj,
      group_var = col,
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = var_comparisons,
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_6_variable_distribution.png"))
    )
    
    plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
      ps_obj,
      group_var = col,
      color_var = col,
      measure = measure,
      comparisons = var_comparisons,
      palette = var_palette,
      title = paste("Variable:", col, "\nAlpha Diversity Distribution")
    )
    
    p6 <- plot_result$plot +
      geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = .data[[col]])) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = var_palette) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
      ) +
      ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
    
    var_plots[[6]] <- p6
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_6_variable_distribution.png")),
      plot = p6,
      width = max(8, length(unique_vals) * 1.5),  # Adjust width based on number of categories
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
  
  cat("  Completed processing for variable:", col, "\n")
}

# Create summary
cat("\n=== PROCESSING COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("All plots have been saved in:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("\nFor each categorical variable, you should find:\n")
cat("1. Variable_1_groups_in_timepoint-1.png\n")
cat("2. Variable_2_groups_in_timepoint-2.png\n")
cat("3. Variable_3_groupA_timepoints.png\n")
cat("4. Variable_4_groupB_timepoints.png\n")
cat("5. Variable_5_groupC_timepoints.png\n")
cat("6. Variable_6_variable_distribution.png\n")
cat("\nFor each plot, two CSV files are also saved:\n")
cat("1. [plotname]_alpha_data.csv - Alpha diversity values for each sample\n")
cat("2. [plotname]_stats.csv - Statistical test results with p-values\n")
cat("\nColor scheme:\n")
cat("- Group A: Light red (#FF9999) for -1, Red (#FF3333) for -2\n")
cat("- Group B: Light blue (#99CCFF) for -1, Blue (#3366FF) for -2\n")
cat("- Group C: Light green (#99FF99) for -1, Green (#33CC33) for -2\n")
```

# other alpha-div metrics
```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load required libraries
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
library(vegan)
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(tools)
library(RColorBrewer)   # for colour palettes (fallback)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Define the alpha diversity measures to iterate over
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
measures <- c("Shannon", "Chao1", "Simpson", "InvSimpson", "Observed")

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Function: plot_alpha_div_custom (now with error handling)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_alpha_div_custom <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    color_var = NULL,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    palette = NULL,
    title = ""
) {
  if (is.null(color_var)) {
    color_var <- group_var
  }
  
  # Calculate alpha diversity with error handling
  alpha_df <- tryCatch({
    df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
    # Ensure it's a data frame with one column
    if (is.null(df) || ncol(df) == 0) {
      stop("microbiome::alpha returned empty result")
    }
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  }, error = function(e) {
    # Fallback to estimate_richness
    message("  Note: microbiome::alpha failed for ", measure, 
            ", using estimate_richness() instead.")
    alpha_est <- estimate_richness(ps_obj, measures = measure)
    df <- data.frame(alpha_est)
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  })
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variables to factors if they're not already
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[color_var]])) {
    meta_df[[color_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[color_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Create the base plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes_string(x = group_var, y = measure, color = color_var)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = group_var,
         y = paste(measure, "Diversity"))
  
  # Add comparisons if provided - REVERTED TO STANDARD STARS
  if (!is.null(comparisons)) {
    # Ensure comparisons are in the right format (as characters, not integers)
    comparisons <- lapply(comparisons, function(x) {
      as.character(x)
    })
    
    # Add standard significance stars (no p-values)
    p <- p + ggpubr::stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = comparisons, 
      method = "wilcox.test", 
      label = "p.signif",  # Just stars
      size = 5,
      step.increase = 0.1,
      tip.length = 0.02
    )
  }
  
  # Add palette if provided
  if (!is.null(palette)) {
    p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = palette)
  }
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = plot_df))
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Function: save_statistics (robust version with tryCatch)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
save_statistics <- function(
    ps_obj,
    group_var,
    measure = 'Shannon',
    comparisons = NULL,
    filename
) {
  # Calculate alpha diversity - with error handling
  alpha_df <- tryCatch({
    df <- microbiome::alpha(ps_obj, index = measure)
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("  WARNING: microbiome::alpha() failed, using estimate_richness()\n")
    alpha_est <- estimate_richness(ps_obj, measures = measure)
    df <- data.frame(alpha_est)
    colnames(df) <- measure
    df
  })
  
  # Get sample data
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # Convert grouping variable to factor if needed
  if (!is.factor(meta_df[[group_var]])) {
    meta_df[[group_var]] <- factor(meta_df[[group_var]])
  }
  
  # Combine alpha diversity with metadata
  plot_df <- cbind(alpha_df, meta_df)
  
  # Save alpha diversity data
  alpha_data_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_alpha_data.csv")
  write.csv(plot_df, alpha_data_file, row.names = TRUE)
  cat("  Saved alpha diversity data:", alpha_data_file, "\n")
  
  # Perform statistical tests if comparisons are provided
  if (!is.null(comparisons) && length(comparisons) > 0) {
    stats_results <- data.frame()
    
    for (i in seq_along(comparisons)) {
      comp <- comparisons[[i]]
      group1 <- as.character(comp[1])
      group2 <- as.character(comp[2])
      
      # Check if groups exist in data
      if (!group1 %in% plot_df[[group_var]] || !group2 %in% plot_df[[group_var]]) {
        cat("  WARNING: Skipping comparison", group1, "vs", group2, "- groups not in data\n")
        next
      }
      
      # Extract data for the two groups
      data1 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group1, measure]
      data2 <- plot_df[plot_df[[group_var]] == group2, measure]
      
      # Perform Wilcoxon test
      if (length(data1) > 1 && length(data2) > 1) {
        test_result <- tryCatch({
          wilcox.test(data1, data2, exact = FALSE)
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat("  ERROR in wilcox.test for", group1, "vs", group2, ":", e$message, "\n")
          NULL
        })
        
        if (!is.null(test_result)) {
          stats_row <- data.frame(
            Comparison = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
            Group1 = group1,
            Group2 = group2,
            N_Group1 = length(data1),
            N_Group2 = length(data2),
            Mean_Group1 = mean(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
            Mean_Group2 = mean(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
            SD_Group1 = sd(data1, na.rm = TRUE),
            SD_Group2 = sd(data2, na.rm = TRUE),
            W_statistic = test_result$statistic,
            P_value = test_result$p.value,
            P_value_rounded = round(test_result$p.value, 4),
            Significance = ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                                  ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                         ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
          )
          
          stats_results <- rbind(stats_results, stats_row)
        }
      } else {
        cat("  WARNING: Not enough data for comparison", group1, "vs", group2, "\n")
      }
    }
    
    # Save statistics results
    if (nrow(stats_results) > 0) {
      stats_file <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), "_stats.csv")
      write.csv(stats_results, stats_file, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("  Saved statistics results:", stats_file, "\n")
    } else {
      cat("  WARNING: No statistical results to save\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(plot_df)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Read the phyloseq object (adjust the path if needed)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

# Prepare the object as in your original code
meta <- sample_data(ps)
meta$Batch <- as.factor(meta$Batch)
ps_obj <- ps
level <- 'Species'
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100

# Filter taxa (core microbiome)
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Glom to species level (using speedyseq if available, otherwise phyloseq)
if (requireNamespace("speedyseq", quietly = TRUE)) {
  ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
} else {
  message("speedyseq not installed; using phyloseq::tax_glom (may drop NAs differently)")
  ps_obj <- tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
}

# Define categorical variables to loop over
categorical_vars <- names(meta)[sapply(meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age', "Group", 'BMI', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 
                                                'Weight', "SampleID"))

# Convert to factors
for (col in categorical_vars) {
  if (col %in% colnames(meta) && (is.character(meta[[col]]) || is.integer(meta[[col]]))) {
    sample_data(ps_obj)[[col]] <- as.factor(sample_data(ps_obj)[[col]])
  }
}

# Ensure Group and Timepoint are factors
sample_data(ps_obj)$Timepoint <- factor(as.character(sample_data(ps_obj)$Timepoint))
sample_data(ps_obj)$Group <- factor(sample_data(ps_obj)$Group)

# Get unique groups and timepoints
groups <- as.factor(sort(unique(sample_data(ps_obj)$Group)))
timepoints <- as.factor(sort(unique(sample_data(ps_obj)$Timepoint)))

# Define colour palettes (unchanged)
groupA_colors <- c("-1" = "#FF9999", "-2" = "#FF3333")
groupB_colors <- c("-1" = "#99CCFF", "-2" = "#3366FF")
groupC_colors <- c("-1" = "#99FF99", "-2" = "#33CC33")
group_palette_time1 <- c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#99CCFF", "C" = "#99FF99")
group_palette_time2 <- c("A" = "#FF3333", "B" = "#3366FF", "C" = "#33CC33")
timepoint_palettes <- list("A" = groupA_colors, "B" = groupB_colors, "C" = groupC_colors)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Outer loop over alpha diversity measures
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
for (measure in measures) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Processing measure:", measure, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n")
  
  # Create measure‑specific output directory
  base_output_dir <- file.path("/home/sonec_iv/alpha_diversity", measure)
  dir.create(base_output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Loop through each categorical variable
  for (col in categorical_vars) {
    cat("\nProcessing variable:", col, "for measure", measure, "\n")
    
    # Create a subdirectory for this variable inside the measure folder
    var_dir <- file.path(base_output_dir, col)
    dir.create(var_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
    
    # Skip if not enough unique values
    if (length(unique(sample_data(ps_obj)[[col]])) < 2) {
      cat("  Skipping - less than 2 unique values\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Store plots for this variable (optional)
    var_plots <- list()
    
    # PLOT 1: Groups A vs B vs C in Timepoint -1
    cat("  Creating Plot 1: Groups in Timepoint -1\n")
    ps_time1 <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == "-1")
    
    if (nsamples(ps_time1) >= 2 && length(unique(sample_data(ps_time1)$Group)) >= 2) {
      group_comparisons <- lapply(combn(groups, 2, simplify = FALSE), as.vector)
      
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_time1,
        group_var = "Group",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = group_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_1_groups_in_timepoint-1.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_time1,
        group_var = "Group",
        color_var = "Group",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = group_comparisons,
        palette = group_palette_time1,
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nGroups in Timepoint -1")
      )
      
      p1 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Group)) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = group_palette_time1) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[1]] <- p1
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_1_groups_in_timepoint-1.png")),
        plot = p1,
        width = 8,
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    # PLOT 2: Groups A vs B vs C in Timepoint -2
    cat("  Creating Plot 2: Groups in Timepoint -2\n")
    ps_time2 <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == "-2")
    
    if (nsamples(ps_time2) >= 2 && length(unique(sample_data(ps_time2)$Group)) >= 2) {
      # Reuse group_comparisons from Plot 1 (already defined)
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_time2,
        group_var = "Group",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = group_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_2_groups_in_timepoint-2.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_time2,
        group_var = "Group",
        color_var = "Group",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = group_comparisons,
        palette = group_palette_time2,
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nGroups in Timepoint -2")
      )
      
      p2 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Group)) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = group_palette_time2) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[2]] <- p2
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_2_groups_in_timepoint-2.png")),
        plot = p2,
        width = 8,
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    # PLOT 3: Group A - Timepoint -1 vs -2
    cat("  Creating Plot 3: Group A timepoints\n")
    ps_groupA <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "A")
    
    if (nsamples(ps_groupA) >= 2 && length(unique(sample_data(ps_groupA)$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
      time_comparisons <- list(c("-1", "-2"))
      
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_groupA,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_3_groupA_timepoints.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_groupA,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        color_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        palette = timepoint_palettes[["A"]],
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nGroup A: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
      )
      
      p3 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["A"]]) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[3]] <- p3
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_3_groupA_timepoints.png")),
        plot = p3,
        width = 8,
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    # PLOT 4: Group B - Timepoint -1 vs -2
    cat("  Creating Plot 4: Group B timepoints\n")
    ps_groupB <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "B")
    
    if (nsamples(ps_groupB) >= 2 && length(unique(sample_data(ps_groupB)$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_groupB,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_4_groupB_timepoints.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_groupB,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        color_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        palette = timepoint_palettes[["B"]],
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nGroup B: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
      )
      
      p4 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["B"]]) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[4]] <- p4
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_4_groupB_timepoints.png")),
        plot = p4,
        width = 8,
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    # PLOT 5: Group C - Timepoint -1 vs -2
    cat("  Creating Plot 5: Group C timepoints\n")
    ps_groupC <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Group == "C")
    
    if (nsamples(ps_groupC) >= 2 && length(unique(sample_data(ps_groupC)$Timepoint)) >= 2) {
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_groupC,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_5_groupC_timepoints.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_groupC,
        group_var = "Timepoint",
        color_var = "Timepoint",
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = time_comparisons,
        palette = timepoint_palettes[["C"]],
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nGroup C: Timepoint -1 vs -2")
      )
      
      p5 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = Timepoint)) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = timepoint_palettes[["C"]]) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[5]] <- p5
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_5_groupC_timepoints.png")),
        plot = p5,
        width = 8,
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    # PLOT 6: Alpha diversity by the categorical variable itself
    cat("  Creating Plot 6: Variable distribution\n")
    unique_vals <- unique(sample_data(ps_obj)[[col]])
    if (length(unique_vals) >= 2) {
      var_comparisons <- lapply(combn(unique_vals, 2, simplify = FALSE), as.vector)
      
      # Create a colour palette
      n_colors <- length(unique_vals)
      if (requireNamespace("pnw", quietly = TRUE)) {
        # If pnw is installed, use it
        if (n_colors <= 8) {
          var_palette <- pnw::pnw_palette("Sunset2", n_colors, type = "continuous")
        } else {
          base_pal <- pnw::pnw_palette("Sunset2", 8, type = "continuous")
          var_palette <- colorRampPalette(base_pal)(n_colors)
        }
      } else {
        # Fallback: use RColorBrewer or rainbow
        if (n_colors <= 9) {
          var_palette <- brewer.pal(n_colors, "Set1")
        } else {
          var_palette <- rainbow(n_colors)
        }
      }
      names(var_palette) <- unique_vals
      
      stats_data <- save_statistics(
        ps_obj,
        group_var = col,
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = var_comparisons,
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_6_variable_distribution.png"))
      )
      
      plot_result <- plot_alpha_div_custom(
        ps_obj,
        group_var = col,
        color_var = col,
        measure = measure,
        comparisons = var_comparisons,
        palette = var_palette,
        title = paste(measure, "Diversity -", col, "\nDistribution by", col)
      )
      
      p6 <- plot_result$plot +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, aes(fill = .data[[col]])) +
        geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
        geom_jitter(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2, height = 0) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = var_palette) +
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
        ) +
        ylab(paste(measure, "Diversity"))
      
      var_plots[[6]] <- p6
      
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(var_dir, paste0(col, "_6_variable_distribution.png")),
        plot = p6,
        width = max(8, length(unique_vals) * 1.5),
        height = 6,
        dpi = 300
      )
    }
    
    cat("  Completed processing for variable:", col, "\n")
  }
  
  cat("\n--- Finished measure:", measure, "---\n")
}

cat("\n=== ALL MEASURES PROCESSED ===\n")
```
